\\n429: // Events is a list of events fired at a target.\\n430: //\\n431: // Checks to see if each pointer event has a corresponding mouse event in the\\n432: // event array and the two events are in the proper order (pointer event is\\n433: // first).\\n434: \",\"endLine\":434,\"startLine\":428},{\"content\":\"13: \\n14: // https://w3c.github.io/pointerevents/#the-button-property\\n15: // Values for the button property, which indicates the device button whose state\\n16: // change fired the event.\\n17: const ButtonChange = {\\n18: \",\"endLine\":18,\"startLine\":13},{\"content\":\"434: //\\n435: // See https://w3c.github.io/pointerevents/#mapping-for-devices-that-support-hover\\n436: function arePointerEventsBeforeCompatMouseEvents(events) {\\n437:   function arePointerAndMouseEventCompatible(pointerEventName, mouseEventName) {\\n438:     return pointerEventName.startsWith(\\\"pointer\\\")\\n439:       && mouseEventName.startsWith(\\\"mouse\\\")\\n440:       && pointerEventName.substring(7) === mouseEventName.substring(5);\\n441:   }\\n442: \",\"endLine\":442,\"startLine\":434}],\"file\":\"tests/wpt/tests/pointerevents/pointerevent_support.js\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99/-/blob/tests/wpt/tests/pointerevents/pointerevent_support.js\"}]}",

            "content": "63:   await targetPointercancelPromise;\n64:   await targetScrollendPromise;\n65:   await verifyScrollStopped(t, target_div);\n66:   assert_equals(scrollend_count, 1, 'Only one scrollend event should be fired');\n67: }\n68: ",

          {
            "content": "40: \n41:   verifyNoScrollendOnDocument(t);\n42: \n43:   let scrollend_count = 0;\n44:   const scrollend_listener = () => { scrollend_count += 1; };\n45:   target_div.addEventListener(\"scrollend\", scrollend_listener);\n46:   t.add_cleanup(() => { target_div.removeEventListener('scrollend', scrollend_listener); });\n47: \n48: ",
            "endLine": 48,

            "content": "55:   const timeoutMs = 3000;\n56:   const targetPointercancelPromise = waitForPointercancelEvent(t, target_div, timeoutMs);\n57:   const targetScrollendPromise = createScrollendPromiseForTarget(t, target_div, timeoutMs);\n58: \n59:   await touchScrollInTargetSequentiallyWithPause(target_div, move_path);\n60: \n61:   // Because we start scrolling after pointerdown, there is no pointerup, instead the target\n62:   // will receive a pointercancel, so we wait for pointercancel, and then continue.\n63: ",
            "endLine": 63,

      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "38: \n39:   assert_equals(target.scrollX, 0);\n40:   var promiseForScrollX = waitForEvent(\"scroll\", t, target);\n41:   target.scrollTo({left: 10});\n42:   await promiseForScrollX;\n43:   assert_equals(target.scrollX, 10);\n44: \n45:   assert_equals(target.scrollY, 0);\n46:   var promiseForScrollY = waitForEvent(\"scroll\", t, target);\n47:   target.scrollTo({top: 10});\n48:   await promiseForScrollY;\n49:   assert_equals(target.scrollY, 10);\n50: \n51: }, \"scrollX and scrollY should fire scroll event.\");\n52: \n53: ",
            "endLine": 53,
            "startLine": 38
          },
          {
            "content": "88: \n89:   assert_equals(target.scrollX, 0);\n90:   var promiseForScrollX = waitForEvent(\"scroll\", t, target);\n91:   target.scrollTo({left: frameDocEl.scrollWidth});\n92:   await promiseForScrollX;\n93:   assert_greater_than(target.scrollX, 0);\n94: \n95:   assert_equals(target.scrollY, 0);\n96:   var promiseForScrollY = waitForEvent(\"scroll\", t, target);\n97:   target.scrollTo({\n98:     top: frameDocEl.scrollHeight,\n99:     left: target.scrollX\n100:   });\n101:   await promiseForScrollY;\n102:   assert_greater_than(target.scrollY, 0);\n103: \n104:   target.addEventListener(\"scroll\", () => assert_unreached(\"Any scroll event should not be observed\"));\n105: \n106:   target.scrollTo({\n107:     left: target.scrollX + 10,\n108:     top: target.scrollY + 10,\n109:   });\n110: ",

      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "428: \n429: // Events is a list of events fired at a target.\n430: //\n431: // Checks to see if each pointer event has a corresponding mouse event in the\n432: // event array and the two events are in the proper order (pointer event is\n433: // first).\n434: ",
            "endLine": 434,
            "startLine": 428
          },
          {
            "content": "13: \n14: // https://w3c.github.io/pointerevents/#the-button-property\n15: // Values for the button property, which indicates the device button whose state\n16: // change fired the event.\n17: const ButtonChange = {\n18: ",
            "endLine": 18,
            "startLine": 13
          },
          {
            "content": "434: //\n435: // See https://w3c.github.io/pointerevents/#mapping-for-devices-that-support-hover\n436: function arePointerEventsBeforeCompatMouseEvents(events) {\n437:   function arePointerAndMouseEventCompatible(pointerEventName, mouseEventName) {\n438:     return pointerEventName.startsWith(\"pointer\")\n439:       && mouseEventName.startsWith(\"mouse\")\n440:       && pointerEventName.substring(7) === mouseEventName.substring(5);\n441:   }\n442: ",
            "endLine": 442,
            "startLine": 434
          }
        ],
        "file": "tests/wpt/tests/pointerevents/pointerevent_support.js",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99/-/blob/tests/wpt/tests/pointerevents/pointerevent_support.js"
      }
    ]
  }
}

base::cross_process_instant::CrossProcessInstant;\\n9: use bitflags::bitflags;\\n10: use devtools_traits::{TimelineMarker, TimelineMarkerType};\\n11: use dom_struct::dom_struct;\\n12: use embedder_traits::InputEventResult;\\n13: use js::rust::HandleObject;\\n14: use keyboard_types::{Key, NamedKey};\\n15: use script_bindings::codegen::GenericBindings::PointerEventBinding::PointerEventMethods;\\n16: use script_bindings::match_domstring_ascii;\\n17: use stylo_atoms::Atom;\\n18: \\n19: use crate::dom::bindings::callback::ExceptionHandling;\\n20: use crate::dom::bindings::cell::DomRefCell;\\n21: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::EventBinding;\\n22: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::EventBinding::{EventConstants, EventMethods};\\n23: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::NodeBinding::GetRootNodeOptions;\\n24: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::NodeBinding::Node_Binding::NodeMethods;\\n25: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::PerformanceBinding::DOMHighResTimeStamp;\\n26: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::ShadowRootBinding::{\\n27:     ShadowRootMethods, ShadowRootMode,\\n28: };\\n29: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::WindowBinding::WindowMethods;\\n30: use crate::dom::bindings::error::Fallible;\\n31: use crate::dom::bindings::inheritance::Castable;\\n32: use crate::dom::bindings::refcounted::Trusted;\\n33: use crate::dom::bindings::reflector::{DomGlobal, Reflector, reflect_dom_object_with_proto};\\n34: use crate::dom::bindings::root::{Dom, DomRoot, MutNullableDom};\\n35: use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\\n36: use crate::dom::element::Element;\\n37: use crate::dom::eventtarget::{EventListeners, EventTarget, ListenerPhase};\\n38: use crate::dom::globalscope::GlobalScope;\\n39: use crate::dom::html::htmlinputelement::InputActivationState;\\n40: use crate::dom::html::htmlslotelement::HTMLSlotElement;\\n41: use crate::dom::mouseevent::MouseEvent;\\n42: use crate::dom::node::{Node, NodeTraits};\\n43: use crate::dom::shadowroot::ShadowRoot;\\n44: use crate::dom::types::{KeyboardEvent, PointerEvent, UserActivation};\\n45: use crate::dom::virtualmethods::vtable_for;\\n46: use crate::dom::window::Window;\\n47: use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\\n48: use crate::task::TaskOnce;\\n49: \\n50: /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-event>\\n51: #[dom_struct]\\n52: pub(crate) struct Event {\\n53:     reflector_: Reflector,\\n54: \\n55:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-currenttarget>\\n56:     current_target: MutNullableDom<EventTarget>,\\n57: \\n58:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-target>\\n59:     target: MutNullableDom<EventTarget>,\\n60: \\n61:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-type>\\n62:     #[no_trace]\\n63:     type_: DomRefCell<Atom>,\\n64: \\n65:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-eventphase>\\n66:     phase: Cell<EventPhase>,\\n67: \\n68:     /// The various specification-defined flags set on this event.\\n69:     flags: Cell<EventFlags>,\\n70: \\n71:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-cancelable>\\n72:     cancelable: Cell<bool>,\\n73: \\n74:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-bubbles>\\n75:     bubbles: Cell<bool>,\\n76: \\n77:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-istrusted>\\n78:     is_trusted: Cell<bool>,\\n79: \\n80:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-timestamp>\\n81:     #[no_trace]\\n82:     time_stamp: CrossProcessInstant,\\n83: \\n84:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path>\\n85:     path: DomRefCell<Vec<EventPathSegment>>,\\n86: \\n87:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-relatedtarget>\\n88:     related_target: MutNullableDom<EventTarget>,\\n89: }\\n90: \\n91: /// An element on an [event path](https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path)\\n92: #[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\\n93: #[cfg_attr(crown, crown::unrooted_must_root_lint::must_root)]\\n94: pub(crate) struct EventPathSegment {\\n95:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path-invocation-target>\\n96:     invocation_target: Dom<EventTarget>,\\n97: \\n98:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path-invocation-target-in-shadow-tree>\\n99:     invocation_target_in_shadow_tree: bool,\\n100: \\n101:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path-shadow-adjusted-target>\\n102:     shadow_adjusted_target: Option<Dom<EventTarget>>,\\n103: \\n104:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path-relatedtarget>\\n105:     related_target: Option<Dom<EventTarget>>,\\n106: \\n107:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path-root-of-closed-tree>\\n108:     root_of_closed_tree: bool,\\n109: \\n110:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path-slot-in-closed-tree>\\n111:     slot_in_closed_tree: bool,\\n112: }\\n113: \\n114: impl Event {\\n115:     pub(crate) fn new_inherited() -> Event {\\n116:         Event {\\n117:             reflector_: Reflector::new(),\\n118:             current_target: Default::default(),\\n119:             target: Default::default(),\\n120:             type_: DomRefCell::new(atom!(\\\"\\\")),\\n121:             phase: Cell::new(EventPhase::None),\\n122:             flags: Cell::new(EventFlags::empty()),\\n123:             cancelable: Cell::new(false),\\n124:             bubbles: Cell::new(false),\\n125:             is_trusted: Cell::new(false),\\n126:             time_stamp: CrossProcessInstant::now(),\\n127:             path: DomRefCell::default(),\\n128:             related_target: Default::default(),\\n129:         }\\n130:     }\\n131: \\n132:     pub(crate) fn     "content": "1: /* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n2:  * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n3:  * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n4: \n5: use std::cell::Cell;\n6: use std::default::Default;\n7: \n8: use base::cross_process_instant::CrossProcessInstant;\n9: use bitflags::bitflags;\n10: use devtools_traits::{TimelineMarker, TimelineMarkerType};\n11: use dom_struct::dom_struct;\n12: use embedder_traits::InputEventResult;\n13: use js::rust::HandleObject;\n14: use keyboard_types::{Key, NamedKey};\n15: use script_bindings::codegen::GenericBindings::PointerEventBinding::PointerEventMethods;\n16: use script_bindings::match_domstring_ascii;\n17: use stylo_atoms::Atom;\n18: \n19: use crate::dom::bindings::callback::ExceptionHandling;\n20: use crate::dom::bindings::cell::DomRefCell;\n21: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::EventBinding;\n22: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::EventBinding::{EventConstants, EventMethods};\n23: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::NodeBinding::GetRootNodeOptions;\n24: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::NodeBinding::Node_Binding::NodeMethods;\n25: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::PerformanceBinding::DOMHighResTimeStamp;\n26: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::ShadowRootBinding::{\n27:     ShadowRootMethods, ShadowRootMode,\n28: };\n29: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::WindowBinding::WindowMethods;\n30: use crate::dom::bindings::error::Fallible;\n31: use crate::dom::bindings::inheritance::Castable;\n32: use crate::dom::bindings::refcounted::Trusted;\n33: use crate::dom::bindings::reflector::{DomGlobal, Reflector, reflect_dom_object_with_proto};\n34: use crate::dom::bindings::root::{Dom, DomRoot, MutNullableDom};\n35: use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\n36: use crate::dom::element::Element;\n37: use crate::dom::eventtarget::{EventListeners, EventTarget, ListenerPhase};\n38: use crate::dom::globalscope::GlobalScope;\n39: use crate::dom::html::htmlinputelement::InputActivationState;\n40: use crate::dom::html::htmlslotelement::HTMLSlotElement;\n41: use crate::dom::mouseevent::MouseEvent;\n42: use crate::dom::node::{Node, NodeTraits};\n43: use crate::dom::shadowroot::ShadowRoot;\n44: use crate::dom::types::{KeyboardEvent, PointerEvent, UserActivation};\n45: use crate::dom::virtualmethods::vtable_for;\n46: use crate::dom::window::Window;\n47: use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n48: use crate::task::TaskOnce;\n49: \n50: /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-event>\n51: #[dom_struct]\n52: pub(crate) struct Event {\n53:     reflector_: Reflector,\n54: \n55:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-currenttarget>\n56:     current_target: MutNullableDom<EventTarget>,\n57: \n58:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-target>\n59:     target: MutNullableDom<EventTarget>,\n60: \n61:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-type>\n62:     #[no_trace]\n63:     type_: DomRefCell<Atom>,\n64: \n65:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-eventphase>\n66:     phase: Cell<EventPhase>,\n67: \n68:     /// The various specification-defined flags set on this event.\n69:     flags: Cell<EventFlags>,\n70: \n71:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-cancelable>\n72:     cancelable: Cell<bool>,\n73: \n74:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-bubbles>\n75:     bubbles: Cell<bool>,\n76: \n77:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-istrusted>\n78:     is_trusted: Cell<bool>,\n79: \n80:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-event-timestamp>\n81:     #[no_trace]\n82:     time_stamp: CrossProcessInstant,\n83: \n84:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path>\n85:     path: DomRefCell<Vec<EventPathSegment>>,\n86: \n87:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-relatedtarget>\n88:     related_target: MutNullableDom<EventTarget>,\n89: }\n90: \n91: /// An element on an [event path](https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path)\n92: #[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\n93: #[cfg_attr(crown, crown::unrooted_must_root_lint::must_root)]\n94: pub(crate) struct EventPathSegment {\n95:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path-invocation-target>\n96:     invocation_target: Dom<EventTarget>,\n97: \n98:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path-invocation-target-in-shadow-tree>\n99:     invocation_target_in_shadow_tree: bool,\n100: \n101:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path-shadow-adjusted-target>\n102:     shadow_adjusted_target: Option<Dom<EventTarget>>,\n103: \n104:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path-relatedtarget>\n105:     related_target: Option<Dom<EventTarget>>,\n106: \n107:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path-root-of-closed-tree>\n108:     root_of_closed_tree: bool,\n109: \n110:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#event-path-slot-in-closed-tree>\n111:     slot_in_closed_tree: bool,\n112: }\n113: \n114: impl Event {\n115:     pub(crate) fn new_inherited() -> Event {\n116:         Event {\n117:             reflector_: Reflector::new(),\n118:             current_target: Default::default(),\n119:             target: Default::default(),\n120:             type_: DomRefCell::new(atom!(\"\")),\n121:             phase: Cell::new(EventPhase::None),\n122:             flags: Cell::new(EventFlags::empty()),\n123:             cancelable: Cell::new(false),\n124:             bubbles: Cell::new(false),\n125:             is_trusted: Cell::new(false),\n126:             time_stamp: CrossProcessInstant::now(),\n127:             path: DomRefCell::default(),\n128:             related_target: Default::default(),\n129:         }\n130:     }\n131: \n132:     pub(crate) fn new_uninitialized(global: &GlobalScope, can_gc: CanGc) -> Set event\u2019s initialized flag.\\n179:         self.set_flags(EventFlags::Initialized);\\n180: \\n181:         // Step 2. Unset event\u2019s stop propagation flag, stop immediate propagation flag, and canceled flag.\\n182:         self.unset_flags(EventFlags::StopPropagation);\\n183:         self.unset_flags(EventFlags::StopImmediatePropagation);\\n184:         self.unset_flags(EventFlags::Canceled);\\n185: \\n186:         // This flag isn't in the specification, but we need to unset it anyway.\\n187:         self.unset_flags(EventFlags::Handled);\\n188: \\n189:         // Step 3. Input
{
  "query": "repo:^github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99$ scroll element dispatch event new Atom"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"blocks\":[{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"26: promise_test(async t => {\\n27:   const scroller = document.scrollingElement;\\n28:   const maxScroll = scroller.scrollHeight - scroller.clientHeight;\\n29:   const timeline = new ScrollTimeline({source: scroller});\\n30:   scroller.scrollTo(0, 0);\\n31:   assert_equals(scroller.scrollTop, 0, \\\"verify test pre-condition\\\");\\n32: \\n33:   scroller.scrollBy({top: 100, behavior: 'smooth'});\\n34:   // Wait for the scroll to change.\\n35:   await waitForScrollEvent(document);\\n36:   // The next frame the scroll changes, the scroll-timelines should not be\\n37:   // updated before the scroll events are dispatched. Store the scroll position\\n38:   // from the previous frame to compare the timeline to.\\n39:   let scroll_percentage = (scroller.scrollTop / maxScroll) * 100;\\n40: \\n41:   await waitForScrollEvent(document);\\n42: \\n43: \",\"endLine\":43,\"startLine\":26},{\"content\":\"2: <meta charset=\\\"utf-8\\\">\\n3: <title>ScrollTimeline snapshotting</title>\\n4: <link     });\\n43:     const actions = new test_driver.Actions().scroll(50, 200, 0, 50, { duration: 50 });\\n44:     actions.send();\\n45:     assert_equals(await scrolled, \\\"root\\\", \\\"Incorrect element scrolled\\\");\\n46:   }, \\\"Wheel-scroll over pointer-events: none scroller skips that scroller\\\");\\n47: </script>\\n48: \",\"endLine\":48,\"startLine\":42}],\"file\":\"tests/wpt/tests/pointerevents/pointer-events-none-skip-scroll-will-change.html\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99/-/blob/tests/wpt/tests/pointerevents/pointer-events-none-skip-scroll-will-change.html\"}]}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "blocks": [
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "26: promise_test(async t => {\n27:   const scroller = document.scrollingElement;\n28:   const maxScroll = scroller.scrollHeight - scroller.clientHeight;\n29:   const timeline = new ScrollTimeline({source: scroller});\n30:   scroller.scrollTo(0, 0);\n31:   assert_equals(scroller.scrollTop, 0, \"verify test pre-condition\");\n32: \n33:   scroller.scrollBy({top: 100, behavior: 'smooth'});\n34:   // Wait for the scroll to change.\n35:   await waitForScrollEvent(document);\n36:   // The next frame the scroll changes, the scroll-timelines should not be\n37:   // updated before the scroll events are dispatched. Store the scroll position\n38:   // from the previous frame to compare the timeline to.\n39:   let scroll_percentage = (scroller.scrollTop / maxScroll) * 100;\n40: \n41:   await waitForScrollEvent(document);\n42: \n43: ",
            "endLine": 43,
            "startLine": 26
          },
          {
            "content": "2: <meta charset=\"utf-8\">\n3: <title>ScrollTimeline snapshotting</title>\n4: <link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://drafts.csswg.org/scroll-animations-1/#event-loop\">\n5: <link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/11613\">\n6: ",
            "endLine": 6,
            "startLine": 2
          },
          {
            "content": "44:       timeline.currentTime,\n45:       scroll_percentage,\n46:       'Scroll timeline current time corresponds to the scroll position.');\n47: }, 'ScrollTimeline current time is updated after programmatic animated ' +\n48:    'scroll.');\n49: \n50: ",
            "endLine": 50,
            "startLine": 44
          }
        ],

            "content": "49: \n50:   // Sanity-check: Verify we can scroll using the test-API. The <iframe> with no\n51:   // query arguments would not preventDefault() any events.\n52:   promise_test(async() => {\n53:     window.scrollTo(0, 0);\n54:     await loadUrlInIframe(iframeElement, url);\n55: \n56:     await inject_scroll(\"down\");\n57:     assert_greater_than(window.scrollY, 0, \"Page must have scrolled down.\");\n58: \n59:     await inject_scroll(\"right\");\n60:     assert_greater_than(window.scrollX, 0, \"Page must have scrolled right.\");\n61:   }, \"Verify that the page normally scrolls.\");\n62: \n63:   // Enable 'vertical-scroll': Blocking \"touchstart\" can block scrolling.\n64:   promise_test(async() => {\n65:     window.scrollTo(0, 0);\n66:     setFeatureState(iframeElement, \"vertical-scroll\", \"*\");\n67:     await loadUrlInIframe(iframeElement, url);\n68:     await sendMessageAndGetResponse(iframeElement.contentWindow,\n69:                                     {\n70:                                       event_type: \"touchstart\",\n71: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 74,
            "startLine": 49
          },
          {
            "content": "94: \n95:     await inject_scroll(\"down\");\n96:     assert_greater_than(window.scrollY,\n97:                         0,\n98:                         \"Main frame must scroll vertically.\");\n99: \n100:     // Horizontal scrolling must still be blocked.\n101:     window.scrollTo(0, 0);\n102:     await inject_scroll(\"right\");\n103:     assert_equals(window.scrollX,\n104:                   0,\n105:                   \"Main frame must not scroll horizontally.\");\n106:   }, \"When feature is disabled, calling 'preventDefault' on 'touchstart' does\" +\n107:      \" not block vertical scroll, but still bocks horizontal scroll.\");\n108: \n109:   // Enable 'vertical-scroll': Blocking \"touchmove\" can block scrolling.\n110:   promise_test(async() => {\n111:     // Make sure <window> can scroll both towards right and bottom.\n112:     window.scrollTo(0, 0);\n113:     setFeatureState(iframeElement, \"vertical-scroll\", \"*\");\n114:     await loadUrlInIframe(iframeElement, url);\n115:     await sendMessageAndGetResponse(iframeElement.contentWindow,\n116: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 121,
            "startLine": 94
          },
          {
            "content": "128: \n129:   // Disable 'vertical-scroll': Blocking \"touchmove\" cannot block vertical\n130:   // scroll.\n131:   promise_test(async() => {\n132:     window.scrollTo(0, 0);\n133:     setFeatureState(iframeElement, \"vertical-scroll\", \"'none'\");\n134:     await loadUrlInIframe(iframeElement, url);\n135:     await sendMessageAndGetResponse(iframeElement.contentWindow,\n136:                                     {\n137:                                       event_type: \"touchmove\",\n138:                                       query: \"#blocker\",\n139:                                       prevent: true\n140:                                     });\n141:     await new Promise((r) => {\n142:       window.setTimeout(r, 100);\n143: ",
            "endLine": 143,

          {
            "content": "109: test(() => {\n110:   let log = dispatchEventWithEventLog(n4, n4.target, new Event('my-event', { bubbles: true, composed: true }));\n111:   assert_equals(log.event.target, n4.host1);\n112:   assert_equals(log.event.eventPhase, 0);\n113:   assert_equals(log.event.currentTarget, null);\n114:   assert_equals(log.event.composedPath().length, 0);\n115: }, 'Event properties post dispatch with nested ShadowRoots (composed: true).');\n116: \n117: ",
            "endLine": 117,
            "startLine": 109
          },
          {
            "content": "18: test(() => {\n19:   let log = dispatchEventWithEventLog(n1, n1.target, new Event('my-event', { bubbles: true, composed: true }));\n20:   assert_equals(log.event.target, n1.target);\n21:   assert_equals(log.event.eventPhase, 0);\n22:   assert_equals(log.event.currentTarget, null);\n23:   assert_equals(log.event.composedPath().length, 0);\n24: }, 'Event properties post dispatch without ShadowRoots (composed: true).');\n25: \n26: ",
            "endLine": 26,
            "startLine": 18
          },
          {

\",\"endLine\":26,\"startLine\":23}],\"file\":\"tests/wpt/tests/css/cssom-view/smooth-scroll-in-load-event.html\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99/-/blob/tests/wpt/tests/css/cssom-view/smooth-scroll-in-load-event.html\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"62: \\n63:     const scroll_events = [];\\n64: \\n65:     // Register the scroll handlers on the window, visualViewport, and both\\n66:     // <div> scrollers.\\n67:     {\\n68:       window.onscroll = () => { scroll_events.push('window-attribute'); }\\n69:       window.addEventListener('scroll', () => { scroll_events.push('window-addEventListener'); });\\n70:       window.visualViewport.onscroll = () => { scroll_events.push('visualViewport-attribute'); }\\n71:       window.visualViewport.addEventListener('scroll', () => {\\n72:             scroll_events.push('visualViewport-addEventListener'); });\\n73:       scroller1.addEventListener('scroll',\\n74:           () => { scroll_events.push('scroller1'); });\\n75:       scroller2.addEventListener('scroll',\\n76:           () => { scroll_events.push('scroller2'); });\\n77:     }\\n78: \",\"endLine\":78,\"startLine\":62},{\"content\":\"87: \\n88:     // The scroll events must be delivered in the order they were executed,\\n89:     // scroller1 first, then the viewport (window then visualViewport), then\\n90:     // scroller2.\\n91:     assert_equals(scroll_events.toString(),\\n92:                   'scroller1,' +\\n93:                   'window-attribute,window-addEventListener,' +\\n94:                   'visualViewport-attribute,visualViewport-addEventListener,' +\\n95:                   'scroller2');\\n96: }, \\\"Scroll event ordering\\\");\\n97: \\n98: \",\"endLine\":98,\"startLine\":87},{\"content\":\"51: \\n52: const scroller1 = document.getElementById('scroller1');\\n53: const scroller2 = document.getElementById('scroller2');\\n54: \\n55:         Test Description: This test checks that the appearance of classic\\n15:         scrollbars will cause a resize event to be fired at window.visualViewport.\\n16:     </h4>\\n17: \",\"endLine\":17,\"startLine\":14},{\"content\":\"33:         var t = async_test(\\n34:             \\\"Resize event was fired at window.visualViewport if, and only if, \\\" +\\n35:             "content": "29: promise_test(async (t) => {\n30:   var target = setupTarget();\n31: \n32:   assert_equals(target.scrollTop, 0);\n33:   var promiseForScrollTop = waitForEvent(\"scroll\", t, target);\n34:   target.scrollTop = 10;\n35:   await promiseForScrollTop;\n36:   assert_equals(target.scrollTop, 10);\n37: \n38:   assert_equals(target.scrollLeft, 0);\n39:   var promiseForScrollLeft = waitForEvent(\"scroll\", t, target);\n40:   target.scrollLeft = 10;\n41:   await promiseForScrollLeft;\n42:   assert_equals(target.scrollLeft, 10);\n43: \n44: }, \"scrollTop and scrollLeft should fire scroll event.\");\n45: \n46: ",
            "endLine": 46,
            "startLine": 29

26. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Find scroll event implementation in Servo",
  "prompt": "Find where scroll events are created and fired in Servo's codebase. Search for:\n1. Where Event objects are created with type=\"scroll\" or atom!(scroll)\n2. Where scroll events are dispatched/fired\n3. The implementation of scrollTop/scrollLeft setters that fire scroll events\n4. Look in components/script/dom and components/script for the scroll event firing logic\n\nUse keyword searches and file browsing to trace the code path.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "abf4dcac7d4851f5b",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary of my findings.\n\n## Summary: Scroll Events in Servo's Codebase\n\nI've successfully traced the scroll event creation and firing logic in Servo. Here are the key findings:\n\n### 1. **Scroll Event Creation and Queuing** (`components/script/dom/document_event_handler.rs` lines 1660-1686)\n\nWhen a scroll is triggered:\n```rust\npub(crate) fn handle_embedder_scroll_event(&self, scrolled_node: ExternalScrollId) {\n    let document = self.window.Document();\n    if scrolled_node.is_root() {\n        document.handle_viewport_scroll_event();\n    } else {\n        // ... find the element and call:\n        document.handle_element_scroll_event(&element);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### 2. **Pending Scroll Event Targets** (`components/script/dom/document.rs` lines 1746-1801)\n\nScroll events are initially added to a pending list:\n\n**For viewport/document scrolling:**\n```rust\npub(crate) fn handle_viewport_scroll_event(&self) {\n    // Step 3: Check if already pending\n    // Step 4: Append document to pending_scroll_event_targets\n    self.pending_scroll_event_targets.borrow_mut().push(Dom::from_ref(target));\n}\n```\n\n**For element scrolling:**\n```rust\npub(crate) fn handle_element_scroll_event(&self, element: &Element) {\n    // Step 3: Check if already pending\n    // Step 4: Append element to pending_scroll_event_targets\n    self.pending_scroll_event_targets.borrow_mut().push(Dom::from_ref(target));\n}\n```\n\n### 3. **Scroll Event Firing** (`components/script/dom/document.rs` lines 1713-1742)\n\nDuring the \"update the rendering\" phase, scroll events are actually fired:\n\n```rust\npub(crate) fn run_the_scroll_steps(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {\n    // Step 2: For each target in pending_scroll_event_targets:\n    let notify_list = self.pending_scroll_event_targets.take();\n    for target in notify_list.iter() {\n        if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\n            // Step 2.1: Fire bubbling scroll event for Document\n            target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n        } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\n            // Step 2.2: Fire non-bubbling scroll event for Element\n            target.fire_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n        }\n    }\n    // Step 3: Clear pending_scroll_event_targets (handled by take())\n}\n```\n\n### 4. **When Scroll Steps are Called** (`components/script/script_thread.rs` lines 1201-1202)\n\nDuring the HTML event loop's \"update the rendering\" task:\n\n```rust\n// Step 9: For each doc of docs, run the scroll steps for doc.\ndocument.run_the_scroll_steps(CanGc::from_cx(cx));\n```\n\n### 5. **ScrollTop/ScrollLeft Setters** (`components/script/dom/element.rs` lines 3420-3469)\n\nWhen scripts set `scrollTop` or `scrollLeft`:\n\n```rust\nfn SetScrollTop(&self, y_: f64) {\n    let behavior = ScrollBehavior::Auto;\n    // ... validation ...\n    // Calls scroll_an_element or window.scroll()\n    win.scroll_an_element(self, self.ScrollLeft() as f32, y, behavior);\n}\n```\n\nWhich eventually triggers:\n```rust\npub(crate) fn scroll_to(&self, position: LayoutVector2D, behavior: ScrollBehavior) {\n    match &self.target {\n        ScrollingBoxSource::Element(element) => {\n            element.owner_window().scroll_an_element(element, position.x, position.y, behavior);\n        },\n        ScrollingBoxSource::Viewport(document) => {\n            document.window().scroll(position.x, position.y, behavior);\n        },\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### 6. **Key Data Structures**\n\n- **Pending scroll event targets**: `pending_scroll_event_targets: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<EventTarget>>>` (Document.rs line 583)\n- **Event firing methods**: `fire_event()`, `fire_bubbling_event()` in EventTarget (eventtarget.rs lines 891-922)\n- **Scroll event type**: Created with atom name `\"scroll\"`\n- **Bubbling behavior**: Document scroll events bubble, Element scroll events do not\n\n### 7. **Flow Diagram**\n\n1. Embedder/user triggers scroll \u2192 `handle_embedder_scroll_event()`\n2. Adds target to `pending_scroll_event_targets` list\n3. During rendering update \u2192 `run_the_scroll_steps()` called\n4. For each pending target:\n   - If Document: fire bubbling \"scroll\" event\n   - If Element: fire non-bubbling \"scroll\" event\n5. Event dispatches to listeners\n\nAll key files are located in:\n- `/workspace/components/script/dom/document.rs` (main scroll event firing logic)\n- `/workspace/components/script/dom/document_event_handler.rs` (scroll event handling)\n- `/workspace/components/script/dom/element.rs` (scrollTop/scrollLeft setters)\n- `/workspace/components/script/dom/scrolling_box.rs` (scroll position management)\n- `/workspace/components/script/script_thread.rs` (event loop integration)",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Find where scroll events are created and fired in Servo's codebase. Search for:\n1. Where Event objects are created with type=\"scroll\" or atom!(scroll)\n2. Where scroll events are dispatched/fired\n3. The implementation of scrollTop/scrollLeft setters that fire scroll events\n4. Look in components/script/dom and components/script for the scroll event firing logic\n\nUse keyword searches and file browsing to trace the code path.",

"{\"content\":\"3420: \\n3421:     // https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-element-scrolltop\\n3422:     // TODO(stevennovaryo): Need to update the scroll API to follow the spec since it is quite outdated.\\n3423:     fn SetScrollTop(&self, y_: f64) {\\n3424:         let behavior = ScrollBehavior::Auto;\\n3425: \\n3426:         // Step 1, 2\\n3427:         let y = if y_.is_finite() { y_ } else { 0.0 } as f32;\\n3428: \\n3429:         let node = self.upcast::<Node>();\\n3430: \\n3431:         // Step 3\\n3432:         let doc = node.owner_doc();\\n3433: \\n3434:         // Step 4\\n3435:         if !doc.is_fully_active() {\\n3436:             return;\\n3437:         }\\n3438: \\n3439:         // Step 5\\n3440:         let win = match doc.GetDefaultView() {\\n3441:             None => return,\\n3442:             Some(win) => win,\\n3443:         };\\n3444: \\n3445:         // Step 7\\n3446:         if self.is_document_element() {\\n3447:             if doc.quirks_mode() != QuirksMode::Quirks {\\n3448:                 win.scroll(win.ScrollX() as f32, y, behavior);\\n3449:             }\\n3450: \\n3451:             return;\\n3452:         }\\n3453: \\n3454:         // Step 9\\n3455:         if doc.GetBody().as_deref() == self.downcast::<HTMLElement>() &&\\n3456:             doc.quirks_mode() == QuirksMode::Quirks &&\\n3457:             !self.is_potentially_scrollable_body()\\n3458:         {\\n3459:             win.scroll(win.ScrollX() as f32, y, behavior);\\n3460:             return;\\n3461:         }\\n3462: \\n3463:         // Step 10\\n3464:         if !self.has_css_layout_box() || !self.has_scrolling_box() || !self.has_overflow() {\\n3465:             return;\\n3466:         }\\n3467: \\n3468:         // Step 11\\n3469:         win.scroll_an_element(self, self.ScrollLeft() as f32, y, behavior);\\n3470:     }\\n3471: \\n3472:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-element-scrollleft>\\n3473:     fn ScrollLeft(&self) -> f64 {\\n3474:         let node = self.upcast::<Node>();\\n3475: \\n3476:         // Step 1\\n3477:         let doc = node.owner_doc();\\n3478: \\n3479:         // Step 2\\n3480:   target.addEventListener('foopy', this.step_func(handler3), false);\\n45:   var ev = new Event('foopy', {bubbles: true});\\n46:   target.dispatchEvent(ev);\\n47:   assert_equals(sawBubble, // Capture: this should fire before both bubbles\\n26:     assert_equals(ev.eventPhase, ev.AT_TARGET);\\n27:     assert_equals(sawCapture, false);\\n28:     assert_equals(sawBubble, false);\\n29: var sawBubbleTwice = false;\\n15:   function handler(ev) {\\n16:     // Added first, but it's a bubble so it shouldn't fire until\\n17:     // after the capture.\\n18:     assert_equals(ev.eventPhase, ev.AT_TARGET);\\n19:     assert_equals(sawCapture, true);\\n20:     assert_equals(sawBubble, false);\\n21:     assert_equals(sawBubbleTwice, false);\\n22:     sawBubble = true;\\n23:   }\\n24:     ) -> DomRoot<Self> {\\n231:         // These values come from the event tables in\\n232:         // <https://w3c.github.io/uievents/#events-mouse-types>.\\n233:         let (bubbles, cancelable, composed) = match event_name {\\n234:             FireMouseEventType::Move | FireMouseEventType::Over | FireMouseEventType::Out => {\\n235:                 (EventBubbles::Bubbles, EventCancelable::Cancelable, true)\\n236:             },\\n237:             FireMouseEventType::Enter | FireMouseEventType::Leave => (\\n238:                 EventBubbles::DoesNotBubble,\\n239: var target_div = document.getElementById('targetDiv');\\n30: var window_received_overscroll = false;\\n31: \\n32: function onOverscroll(event) {\\n33:   assert_false(event.cancelable);\\n34:   // overscroll events targetting document are bubbled to the window.\\n35:   assert_true(event.bubbles);\\n36:   window_received_overscroll = true;\\n37: }\\n38: window.addEventListener(\\\"overscroll\\\", onOverscroll);\\n39: \\n40: \",\"endLine\":40,\"startLine\":29},{\"content\":\"41:   promise_test (async (t) => {\\n42:     // Make sure that no overscroll event is sent to target_div.\\n43:     target_div.addEventListener(\\\"overscroll\\\",\\n44:         t.unreached_func(\\\"target_div got unexpected overscroll event.\\\"));\\n45:     // Scroll up on target div and wait for the window to get overscroll event.\\n46:     await touchScrollInTarget(300, target_div, 'up');\\n47:     await waitFor(() => { return window_received_overscroll; },\\n48:         'Window did not receive overscroll event after scroll up on target.');\\n49:   }, 'Tests that the window gets overscroll event when no element scrolls' +\\n50:      'after touch scrolling.');\\n51: fetchResultsNoResolver(key);\\n30:   assert_true(results.beforematchFiredOnFoo,\\n31:     'Foo was searched for, so it should get a beforematch event.');\\n32: "{\"content\":\"1660: \\n1661:     /// Handle a scroll event triggered by user interactions from the embedder.\\n1662:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events>\\n1663:     #[expect(unsafe_code)]\\n1664:     pub(crate) fn handle_embedder_scroll_event(&self, scrolled_node: ExternalScrollId) {\\n1665:         // If it is a viewport scroll.\\n1666:         let document = self.window.Document();\\n1667:         if scrolled_node.is_root() {\\n1668:             document.handle_viewport_scroll_event();\\n1669:         } else {\\n1670:             // Otherwise, check whether it is for a relevant element within the document.\\n1671:             let Some(node_id) = node_id_from_scroll_id(scrolled_node.0 as usize) else {\\n1672:                 return;\\n1673:             };\\n1674:             let node = unsafe {\\n1675:                 node::from_untrusted_node_address(UntrustedNodeAddress::from_id(node_id))\\n1676:             };\\n1677:             let Some(element) = node\\n1678:                 .inclusive_ancestors(ShadowIncluding::Yes)\\n1679:                 .find_map(DomRoot::downcast::<Element>)\\n1680:             else {\\n1681:                 return;\\n1682:             };\\n1683: \\n1684:             document.handle_element_scroll_event(&element);\\n1685:         }\\n1686:     }\\n1687: \\n1688:     pub(crate) fn run_default_keyboard_event_handler(&self, event: &KeyboardEvent) {\\n1689:         if event.upcast::<Event>().type_() != atom!(\\\"keydown\\\") {\\n1690:             return;\\n1691:         }\\n1692:         if !event.modifiers().is_empty() {\\n1693:             return;\\n1694:         }\\n1695:         let scroll = match event.key() {\\n1696:             Key::Named(NamedKey::ArrowDown) => KeyboardScroll::Down,\\n1697:             Key::Named(NamedKey::ArrowLeft) => KeyboardScroll::Left,\\n1698:             Key::Named(NamedKey::ArrowRight) => KeyboardScroll::Right,\\n1699:             Key::Named(NamedKey::ArrowUp) => KeyboardScroll::Up,\\n1700:             Key::Named(NamedKey::End) => KeyboardScroll::End,\\n1701: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/servo/servo@b590c6c8487d3d682005a6b3469124909103d4d0/-/blob/components/script/dom/document_event_handler.rs%3FL1660-1700\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1746:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events>\\n1747:     pub(crate) fn handle_viewport_scroll_event(&self) {\\n1748:         // Step 2.\\n1749:         // > If doc is a snap container, run the steps to update scrollsnapchanging targets\\n1750:         // > for doc with doc\u2019s eventual snap target in the block axis as newBlockTarget and\\n1751:         // > doc\u2019s eventual snap target in the inline axis as newInlineTarget.\\n1752:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\\n1753: \\n1754:         // Step 3.\\n1755:         // > If doc is already in doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets, abort these steps.\\n1756:         let target = self.upcast::<EventTarget>();\\n1757:         if self\\n1758:             .pending_scroll_event_targets\\n1759:             .borrow()\\n1760:             .iter()\\n1761:             .any(|other_target| *other_target == target)\\n1762:         {\\n1763:             return;\\n1764:         }\\n1765: \\n1766:         // Step 4.\\n1767:         // > Append doc to doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets.\\n1768:         self.pending_scroll_event_targets\\n1769:             .borrow_mut()\\n1770:             .push(Dom::from_ref(target));\\n1771:     }\\n1772: \\n1773:     /// Whenever an element gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an\\n1774:     /// API), the user agent must run these steps:\\n1775:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events>\\n1776:     pub(crate) fn handle_element_scroll_event(&self, element: &Element) {\\n1777:         // Step 2.\\n1778:         // > If the element is a snap container, run the steps to update scrollsnapchanging\\n1779:         // > targets for the element with the element\u2019s eventual snap target in the block\\n1780:         // > axis as newBlockTarget and the element\u2019s eventual snap target in the inline axis\\n1781:         // > as newInlineTarget.\\n1782:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\\n1783: \\n1784:         // Step 3.\\n1785:         // > If the element is already in doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets, abort these steps.\\n1786:         let target = element.upcast::<EventTarget>();\\n1787:         if self\\n1788:             .pending_scroll_event_targets\\n1789:             .borrow()\\n1790:             .iter()\\n1791:             .any(|other_target| *other_target == target)\\n1792:         {\\n1793:             return;\\n1794:         }\\n1795: \\n1796:         // Step 4.\\n1797:         // > Append the element to doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets.\\n1798:         self.pending_scroll_event_targets\\n1799:             .borrow_mut()\\n1800:             .push(Dom::from_ref(target));\\n1801:     }\\n1802: \\n1803:     // https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#converting-nodes-into-a-node\\n1804:     pub(crate) fn node_from_nodes_and_strings(\\n1805:         &self,\\n1806:         mut nodes: Vec<NodeOrString>,\\n1807:         can_gc: CanGc,\\n1808:     ) -> Fallible<DomRoot<Node>> {\\n1809:         if nodes.len() == 1 {\\n1810:             Ok(match nodes.pop().unwrap() {\\n1811:                 NodeOrString::Node(node) => node,\\n1812:                 NodeOrString::String(string) => {\\n1813:                     DomRoot::upcast(self.CreateTextNode(string, can_gc))\\n1814:                 },\\n1815:             })\\n1816:         } else {\\n1817:             let fragment = DomRoot::upcast::<Node>(self.CreateDocumentFragment(can_gc));\\n1818:             for node in nodes {\\n1819:                 match node {\\n1820:                     NodeOrString::Node(node) => {\\n1821: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/servo/servo@b590c6c8487d3d682005a6b3469124909103d4d0/-/blob/components/script/dom/document.rs%3FL1746-1820\"}"

    pub pipelines: FxHashMap<PipelineId, PipelineDetails>,\\n92:     /// Pending scroll/zoom events.\\n93:     pending_scroll_zoom_events: Vec<ScrollZoomEvent>,\\n94:     /// A map of pending wheel So, browsers should\\n243:     // not dispatch `pointerrawupdate` event and the \\\"fire a pointer event\\\" steps\\n244:     // including the \\\"process pending pointer capture\\\" steps should not run twice\\n245:     // per `pointermove`.\\n246:     assert_true(true, \\\"There is no `pointerrawupdate` event listener anymore\\\");\\n247:   });\\n248: \",\"endLine\":248,\"startLine\":240},{\"content\":\"28: /**\\n29:  * `pointerrawupdate` is defined as:\\n30:  * > The user agent MUST fire a pointer event named pointerrawupdate, and only\\n31:  * > do so within a secure context, when a pointer changes any properties that\\n32:  * > don't fire pointerdown or pointerup events.\\n33:  *\\n34:  * The following `pointermove` event is also defined as:\\n35:  * > The user agent MUST fire a pointer event named pointermove\\n36:  *\\n37:  * So, when a set of `pointerrawupdate` and `pointermove` is dispatched, the\\n38:  * \\\"fire a pointer event\\\" runs twice.\\n39:  *\\n40:  * \\\"fire a pointer event\\\" defines:\\n41:  * > If the event is not a gotpointercapture, lostpointercapture, click,\\n42:  * > auxclick or contextmenu event, run the process pending pointer capture\\n43:  * > steps for this PointerEvent.\\n44:  *\\n45: \",\"endLine\":45,\"startLine\":28},{\"content\":\"6: <meta name=\\\"viewport\\\" content=\\\"width=device-width, initial-scale:1, user-scalable=no\\\">\\n7: <title>Test for handling of \\\"fire a pointer event named pointerrawupdate\\\"</title>\\n8: <script 52. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_keyword_search @ -
Input
{
  "query": "repo:^github.com/servo/servo$ update_the_rendering scroll pending targets"
}

renderer has asked us for a new rendering update.\\n611:     pub(crate) fn set_needs_rendering_update(&self) {\\n612:         self.needs_rendering_update.store(true, Ordering::Relaxed);\\n613:     }\\n614: \",\"endLine\":614,\"startLine\":608},{\"content\":\"387: \\n388:     /// The [`TimerId`] of a ScriptThread-scheduled \\\"update the rendering\\\" call, if any.\\n389:     /// The ScriptThread schedules calls to \\\"update the rendering,\\\" but the renderer can\\n390:     /// also do this when animating. Renderer-based calls always take precedence.\\n391:     #[no_trace]\\n392:     scheduled_update_the_rendering: RefCell<Option<TimerId>>,\\n393: \\n394:     /// Whether an animation tick or ScriptThread-triggered rendering update is pending. This might\\n395:     /// either be because the Servo renderer is managing animations and the [`ScriptThread`] has\\n396:     /// received a [`ScriptThreadMessage::TickAllAnimations`] message, because the [`ScriptThread`]\\n397:     /// itself is managing animations the timer fired triggering a [`ScriptThread`]-based\\n398:     /// animation tick, or if there are no animations running and the [`ScriptThread`] has noticed a\\n399:     /// change that requires a rendering update.\\n400:     needs_rendering_update: Arc<AtomicBool>,\\n401: \\n402: \",\"endLine\":402,\"startLine\":387},{\"content\":\"1103: \\n1104:     fn cancel_scheduled_update_the_rendering(&self) {\\n1105:         if let Some(timer_id) = self.scheduled_update_the_rendering.borrow_mut().take() {\\n1106:             self.timer_scheduler.borrow_mut().cancel_timer(timer_id);\\n1107: \",\"endLine\":1107,\"startLine\":1103}],\"file\":\"components/script/script_thread.rs\",\"repo\":\"github.com/servo/servo\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/servo/servo/-/blob/components/script/script_thread.rs\"}]}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"1715:         // Step 1.\\n1716:         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchanging events for doc.\\n1717:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\\n1718: \\n1719:         // Step 2\\n1720:         // > For each item target in doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets, in the order they\\n1721:         // > were added to the list, run these substeps:\\n1722:         // Step 3.\\n1723:         // > Empty doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets.\\n1724:         // Since the scroll event callback could trigger another scroll event, we are taking all of the\\n1725:         // current scroll event to avoid borrow checking error.\\n1726:         rooted_vec!(let notify_list <- self.pending_scroll_event_targets.take().into_iter());\\n1727:         for target in notify_list.iter() {\\n1728:             if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\\n1729:                 // Step 2.1\\n1730:                 // > If target is a Document, fire an event named scroll that bubbles at target.\\n1731:                 target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\\\"scroll\\\"), can_gc);\\n1732:             } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\\n1733:                 // Step 2.2\\n1734:                 // > Otherwise, fire an event named scroll at target.\\n1735:                 target.fire_event(Atom::from(\\\"scroll\\\"), can_gc);\\n1736:             }\\n1737:         }\\n1738: \\n1739:         // Step 4.\\n1740:         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchange events for doc.\\n1741:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\\n1742:     }\\n1743: \\n1744:     /// Whenever a viewport gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an\\n1745:     /// API), the user agent must run these steps:\\n1746: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/servo/servo@b590c6c8487d3d682005a6b3469124909103d4d0/-/blob/components/script/dom/document.rs%3FL1715-1745\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1180:             }\\n1181: \\n1182:             if document.waiting_on_canvas_image_updates() {\\n1183:                 continue;\\n1184:             }\\n1185: \\n1186:             // Clear this as early as possible so that any callbacks that\\n1187:             // trigger new reasons for updating the rendering don't get lost.\\n1188:             document.clear_rendering_update_reasons();\\n1189: \\n1190:             // TODO(#31581): The steps in the \\\"Revealing the document\\\" section need to be implemented\\n1191:             // `process_pending_input_events` handles the focusing steps as well as other events\\n1192:             // from `Paint`.\\n1193: \\n1194:             // TODO: Should this be broken and to match the specification more closely? For instance see\\n1195:             // https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#flush-autofocus-candidates.\\n1196:             self.process_pending_input_events(*pipeline_id, CanGc::from_cx(cx));\\n1197: \\n1198:             // > 8. For each doc of docs, run the resize steps for doc. [CSSOMVIEW]\\n1199:             let resized = document.window().run_the_resize_steps(CanGc::from_cx(cx));\\n1200: \\n1201:             // > 9. For each doc of docs, run the scroll steps for doc.\\n1202:             document.run_the_scroll_steps(CanGc::from_cx(cx));\\n1203: \\n1204:             // Media queries is only relevant when there are resizing.\\n1205:             if resized {\\n1206:                 // 10. For each doc of docs, evaluate media queries and report changes for doc.\\n1207:                 document\\n1208:                     .window()\\n1209:                     .evaluate_media_queries_and_report_changes(CanGc::from_cx(cx));\\n1210: \\n1211:                 // https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#img-environment-changes\\n1212:                 // As per the spec, this can be run at any time.\\n1213:                 document.react_to_environment_changes()\\n1214:             }\\n1215: \\n1216:             // > 11. For each doc of docs, update animations and send events for doc, passing\\n1217:             // > in relative high resolution time given frameTimestamp and doc's relevant\\n1218:             // > global object as the timestamp [WEBANIMATIONS]\\n1219:             document.update_animations_and_send_events(cx);\\n1220: \\n1221: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/servo/servo@b590c6c8487d3d682005a6b3469124909103d4d0/-/blob/components/script/script_thread.rs%3FL1180-1220\"}"

euclid::{Rect, Vector2D};\\n9: use layout_api::{AxesOverflow, ScrollContainerQueryFlags};\\n10: use script_bindings::codegen::GenericBindings::WindowBinding::ScrollBehavior;\\n11: use script_bindings::inheritance::Castable;\\n12: use script_bindings::root::DomRoot;\\n13: use style::values::computed::Overflow;\\n14: use style_traits::CSSPixel;\\n15: use NodeTraits};\\n19: use crate::dom::types::{Document, Element};\\n20: \\n21: pub(crate) struct ScrollingBox {\\n22:     target: ScrollingBoxSource,\\n23:     overflow: AxesOverflow,\\n24:     cached_content_size: Cell<Option<LayoutSize>>,\\n25:     cached_size: Cell<Option<LayoutSize>>,\\n26: }\\n27: \\n28: /// Represents a scrolling box that can be either an element or the viewport\\n29: /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-box>\\n30: pub(crate) enum ScrollingBoxSource {\\n31:     Element(DomRoot<Element>),\\n32:     Viewport(DomRoot<Document>),\\n33: }\\n34: \\n35: #[derive(Copy, Clone)]\\n36: pub(crate) enum ScrollingBoxAxis {\\n37:     X,\\n38:     Y,\\n39: }\\n40: \\n41: #[derive(Copy, Clone)]\\n42: pub(crate) enum ScrollRequirement {\\n43:     Always,\\n44:     IfNotVisible,\\n45: }\\n46: \\n47: impl ScrollRequirement {\\n48:     fn compute_need_scroll(\\n49:         &self,\\n50:         element_start: f32,\\n51:         element_end: f32,\\n52:         container_size: f32,\\n53:     ) -> bool {\\n54:         match self {\\n55:             ScrollRequirement::Always => true,\\n56:             ScrollRequirement::IfNotVisible => {\\n57:                 let scrollport_start = 0.;\\n58:                 let scrollport_end = container_size;\\n59: \\n60:                 element_end <= scrollport_start || element_start >= scrollport_end\\n61:             },\\n62:         }\\n63:     }\\n64: }\\n65: \\n66: #[derive(Copy, Clone)]\\n67: pub(crate) struct ScrollAxisState {\\n68:     pub(crate) position: ScrollLogicalPosition,\\n69:     pub(crate) requirement: ScrollRequirement,\\n70: }\\n71: \\n72: impl ScrollAxisState {\\n73:     pub fn new_always_scroll_position(position: ScrollLogicalPosition) -> Self {\\n74:         ScrollAxisState {\\n75:             position,\\n76:             requirement: ScrollRequirement::Always,\\n77:         }\\n78:     }\\n79: }\\n80: \\n81: impl ScrollingBox {\\n82:     pub(crate) fn new(target: ScrollingBoxSource, overflow: AxesOverflow) -> Self {\\n83:         Self {\\n84:             target,\\n85:             overflow,\\n86:             cached_content_size: Default::default(),\\n87:             cached_size: Default::default(),\\n88:         }\\n89:     }\\n90: \\n91:     pub(crate) fn target(&self) -> &ScrollingBoxSource {\\n92:         &self.target\\n93:     }\\n94: \\n95:     pub(crate) fn is_viewport(&self) -> bool {\\n96:         matches!(self.target, ScrollingBoxSource::Viewport(..))\\n97:     }\\n98: \\n99:     pub(crate) fn scroll_position(&self) -> LayoutVector2D {\\n100:         match &self.target {\\n101: "{\"content\":\"160: \\n161:     pub(crate) fn scroll_to(&self, position: LayoutVector2D, behavior: ScrollBehavior) {\\n162:         match &self.target {\\n163:             ScrollingBoxSource::Element(element) => {\\n164:                 element\\n165:                     .owner_window()\\n166:                     .scroll_an_element(element, position.x, position.y, behavior);\\n167:             },\\n168:             ScrollingBoxSource::Viewport(document) => {\\n169:                 document.window().scroll(position.x, position.y, behavior);\\n170:             },\\n171:         }\\n172:     }\\n173: \\n174:     pub(crate) fn can_keyboard_scroll_in_axis(&self, axis: ScrollingBoxAxis) -> bool {\\n175:         let overflow = match axis {\\n176:             ScrollingBoxAxis::X => self.overflow.x,\\n177:             ScrollingBoxAxis::Y => self.overflow.y,\\n178:         };\\n179:         if overflow == Overflow::Hidden {\\n180:             return false;\\n181:         }\\n182:         match axis {\\n183:             ScrollingBoxAxis::X => self.content_size().width > self.size().width,\\n184:             ScrollingBoxAxis::Y => self.content_size().height > self.size().height,\\n185:         }\\n186:     }\\n187: \\n188:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#determine-the-scroll-into-view-position>\\n189:     pub(crate) fn determine_scroll_into_view_position(\\n190:         &self,\\n191:         block: ScrollAxisState,\\n192:         inline: ScrollAxisState,\\n193:         target_rect: Rect<Au, CSSPixel>,\\n194:     ) -> LayoutVector2D {\\n195:         let device_pixel_ratio = self.node().owner_window().device_pixel_ratio().get();\\n196:         let to_pixel = |value: Au| value.to_nearest_pixel(device_pixel_ratio);\\n197: \\n198:         // Step 1 should be handled by the caller, and provided as |target_rect|.\\n199:         // > Let target bounding border box be the box represented by the return value\\n200:         // > of invoking Element\u2019s getBoundingClientRect(), if target is an Element,\\n201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/servo/servo@b590c6c8487d3d682005a6b3469124909103d4d0/-/blob/components/script/dom/scrolling_box.rs%3FL160-200\"}"

target in the inline axis as newInlineTarget.\\n1728:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\\n1729: \\n1730:         // Step 3.\\n1731:         // > If doc is already in doc\u2019s pending scroll snapping\\n1759: \\n1760:         // Step 3.\\n1761:         // > If the element is already in doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets, abort these steps.\\n1762:         let target = can_gc: CanGc,\\n1784:     ) -> Fallible<DomRoot<Node>> {\\n1785:         if nodes.len() == 1 {\\n1786:             Ok(match nodes.pop().unwrap() {\\n1787:                 NodeOrString::Node(node) => node,\\n1788:                 NodeOrString::String(string) => {\\n1789:                     DomRoot::upcast(self.CreateTextNode(string, can_gc))\\n1790:                 },\\n1791: https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#converting-nodes-into-a-node\n1780:     pub(crate) fn node_from_nodes_and_strings(\n1781:         &self,\n1782:         mut nodes: Vec<NodeOrString>,\n1783:         can_gc: CanGc,\n1784:     ) -> Fallible<DomRoot<Node>> {\n1785:         if nodes.len() == 1 {\n1786:             Ok(match nodes.pop().unwrap() {\n1787:                 NodeOrString::Node(node) => node,\n1788:                 NodeOrString::String(string) => {\n1789:                     DomRoot::upcast(self.CreateTextNode(string, can_gc))\n1790:                 },\n1791:             })\n1792:         } else {\n1793:             let fragment = DomRoot::upcast::<Node>(self.CreateDocumentFragment(can_gc));\n1794:             for node in nodes {\n1795:                 match node {\n1796:                     NodeOrString::Node(node) => {\n1797:                         fragment.AppendChild(&node, can_gc)?;\n1798:                     },\n1799:                     NodeOrString::String(string) => {\n1800:                         let node = DomRoot::upcast::<Node>(self.CreateTextNode(string, can_gc));\n1801:                         // No try!() here because appending a text node\n1802:                         // should not fail.\n1803:                         fragment.AppendChild(&node, can_gc).unwrap();\n1804:                     },\n1805:                 }\n1806:             }\n1807:             Ok(fragment)\n1808:         }\n1809:     }\n1810: \n1811: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99@dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c/-/blob/components/script/dom/document.rs%3FL1700-1810"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1690:     pub(crate) fn run_the_scroll_steps(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {\\n1691:         // Step 1.\\n1692:         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchanging events for doc.\\n1693:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\\n1694: \\n1695:         // Step 2\\n1696:         // > For each item target in doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets, in the order they\\n1697:         // > were added to the list, run these substeps:\\n1698:         // Step 3.\\n1699:         // > Empty doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets.\\n1700:         // Since the scroll event callback could trigger another scroll event, we are taking all of the\\n1701:         // current scroll event to avoid borrow checking error.\\n1702:         rooted_vec!(let notify_list <- self.pending_scroll_event_targets.take().into_iter());\\n1703:         for target in notify_list.iter() {\\n1704:             if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\\n1705:                 // Step 2.1\\n1706:                 // > If target is a Document, fire an event named scroll that bubbles at target.\\n1707:                 target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\\\"scroll\\\"), can_gc);\\n1708:             } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\\n1709:                 // Step 2.2\\n1710:                 // > Otherwise, fire an event named scroll at target.\\n1711:                 target.fire_event(Atom::from(\\\"scroll\\\"), can_gc);\\n1712:             }\\n1713:         }\\n1714: \\n1715:         // Step 4.\\n1716:         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchange events for doc.\\n1717:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\\n1718:     }\\n1719: \\n1720:     /// Whenever a viewport gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an\\n1721: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99@dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c/-/blob/components/script/dom/document.rs%3FL1690-1720\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1690:     pub(crate) fn run_the_scroll_steps(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {\n1691:         // Step 1.\n1692:         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchanging events for doc.\n1693:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\n1694: \n1695:         // Step 2\n1696:         // > For each item target in doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets, in the order they\n1697:         // > were added to the list, run these substeps:\n1698:         // Step 3.\n1699:         // > Empty doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets.\n1700:         // Since the scroll event callback could trigger another scroll event, we are taking all of the\n1701:         // current scroll event to avoid borrow checking error.\n1702:         rooted_vec!(let notify_list <- self.pending_scroll_event_targets.take().into_iter());\n1703:         for target in notify_list.iter() {\n1704:             if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\n1705:                 // Step 2.1\n1706:                 // > If target is a Document, fire an event named scroll that bubbles at target.\n1707:                 target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n1708:             } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\n1709:                 // Step 2.2\n1710:                 // > Otherwise, fire an event named scroll at target.\n1711:                 target.fire_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n1712:             }\n1713:         }\n1714: \n1715:         // Step 4.\n1716:         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchange events for doc.\n1717:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\n1718:     }\n1719: \n1720:     /// Whenever a viewport gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an\n1721: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99@dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c/-/blob/components/script/dom/document.rs%3FL1690-1720"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\\n2:  * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\\n3:  * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\\n4: \\n5: //! The set of animations for a document.\\n6: \\n7: use std::cell::Cell;\\n8: \\n9: use base::id::PipelineId;\\n10: use constellation_traits::ScriptToConstellationMessage;\\n11: use cssparser::ToCss;\\n12: use embedder_traits::{AnimationState as AnimationsPresentState, UntrustedNodeAddress};\\n13: use libc::c_void;\\n14: use rustc_hash::{FxHashMap, FxHashSet};\\n15: use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\\n16: use style::animation::{\\n17:     Animation, AnimationSetKey, AnimationState, DocumentAnimationSet, ElementAnimationSet,\\n18:     KeyframesIterationState, Transition,\\n19: };\\n20: use style::dom::OpaqueNode;\\n21: use style::selector_parser::PseudoElement;\\n22: \\n23: use crate::dom::animationevent::AnimationEvent;\\n24: use crate::dom::bindings::cell::DomRefCell;\\n25: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::AnimationEventBinding::AnimationEventInit;\\n26: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::EventBinding::EventInit;\\n27: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::TransitionEventBinding::TransitionEventInit;\\n28: use crate::dom::bindings::inheritance::Castable;\\n29: use crate::dom::bindings::num::Finite;\\n30: use crate::dom::bindings::root::{Dom, DomRoot};\\n31: use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\\n32: use crate::dom::bindings::trace::NoTrace;\\n33: use crate::dom::event::Event;\\n34: use crate::dom::node::{Node, NodeDamage, NodeTraits, from_untrusted_node_address};\\n35: use crate::dom::transitionevent::TransitionEvent;\\n36: use crate::dom::window::Window;\\n37: use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\\n38: \\n39: /// The set of animations for a document.\\n40: #[derive(Default, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\\n41: #[cfg_attr(crown, crown::unrooted_must_root_lint::must_root)]\\n42: pub(crate) struct Animations {\\n43:     /// The map of nodes to their animation states.\\n44:     #[no_trace]\\n45:     pub(crate) sets: DocumentAnimationSet,\\n46: \\n47:     /// Whether or not we have animations that are running.\\n48:     has_running_animations: Cell<bool>,\\n49: \\n50:     /// A list of nodes with in-progress CSS transitions or pending events.\\n51:     rooted_nodes: DomRefCell<FxHashMap<NoTrace<OpaqueNode>, Dom<Node>>>,\\n52: \\n53:     /// A list of pending animation-related events.\\n54:     pending_events: DomRefCell<Vec<TransitionOrAnimationEvent>>,\\n55: \\n56:     /// The timeline value at the last time all animations were marked dirty.\\n57:     /// This is used to prevent marking animations dirty when the timeline\\n58:     /// has not changed.\\n59:     timeline_value_at_last_dirty: Cell<f64>,\\n60: }\\n61: \\n62: impl Animations {\\n63:     pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {\\n64:         Animations {\\n65:             sets: Default::default(),\\n66:             has_running_animations: Cell::new(false),\\n67:             rooted_nodes: Default::default(),\\n68:             pending_events: Default::default(),\\n69:             timeline_value_at_last_dirty: Cell::new(0.0),\\n70:         }\\n71:     }\\n72: \\n73:     pub(crate) fn clear(&self) {\\n74:         self.sets.sets.write().clear();\\n75:         self.rooted_nodes.borrow_mut().clear();\\n76:         self.pending_events.borrow_mut().clear();\\n77:     }\\n78: \\n79:     // Mark all animations dirty, if they haven't been marked dirty since the\\n80:     // specified `current_timeline_value`. Returns true if animations were marked\\n81:     // dirty or false otherwise.\\n82:     pub(crate) fn mark_animating_nodes_as_dirty(&self, current_timeline_value: f64) -> bool {\\n83:         if current_timeline_value <= self.timeline_value_at_last_dirty.get() {\\n84:             return false;\\n85:         }\\n86:         self.timeline_value_at_last_dirty\\n87:             .set(current_timeline_value);\\n88: \\n89:         let sets = self.sets.sets.read();\\n90:         let rooted_nodes = self.rooted_nodes.borrow();\\n91:         for node in sets\\n92:             .keys()\\n93:             .filter_map(|key| rooted_nodes.get(&NoTrace(key.node)))\\n94:         {\\n95:             node.dirty(NodeDamage::Style);\\n96:         }\\n97: \\n98:         true\\n99:     }\\n100: \\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99@dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c/-/blob/components/script/animations.rs%3FL1-100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n2:  * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n3:  * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n4: \n5: //! The set of animations for a document.\n6: \n7: use std::cell::Cell;\n8: \n9: use base::id::PipelineId;\n10: use constellation_traits::ScriptToConstellationMessage;\n11: use cssparser::ToCss;\n12: use embedder_traits::{AnimationState as AnimationsPresentState, UntrustedNodeAddress};\n13: use libc::c_void;\n14: use rustc_hash::{FxHashMap, FxHashSet};\n15: use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n16: use style::animation::{\n17:     Animation, AnimationSetKey, AnimationState, DocumentAnimationSet, ElementAnimationSet,\n18:     KeyframesIterationState, Transition,\n19: };\n20: use style::dom::OpaqueNode;\n21: use style::selector_parser::PseudoElement;\n22: \n23: use crate::dom::animationevent::AnimationEvent;\n24: use crate::dom::bindings::cell::DomRefCell;\n25: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::AnimationEventBinding::AnimationEventInit;\n26: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::EventBinding::EventInit;\n27: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::TransitionEventBinding::TransitionEventInit;\n28: use crate::dom::bindings::inheritance::Castable;\n29: use crate::dom::bindings::num::Finite;\n30: use crate::dom::bindings::root::{Dom, DomRoot};\n31: use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\n32: use crate::dom::bindings::trace::NoTrace;\n33: use crate::dom::event::Event;\n34: use crate::dom::node::{Node, NodeDamage, NodeTraits, from_untrusted_node_address};\n35: use crate::dom::transitionevent::TransitionEvent;\n36: use crate::dom::window::Window;\n37: use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n38: \n39: /// The set of animations for a document.\n40: #[derive(Default, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\n41: #[cfg_attr(crown, crown::unrooted_must_root_lint::must_root)]\n42: pub(crate) struct Animations {\n43:     /// The map of nodes to their animation states.\n44:     #[no_trace]\n45:     pub(crate) sets: DocumentAnimationSet,\n46: \n47:     /// Whether or not we have animations that are running.\n48:     has_running_animations: Cell<bool>,\n49: \n50:     /// A list of nodes with in-progress CSS transitions or pending events.\n51:     rooted_nodes: DomRefCell<FxHashMap<NoTrace<OpaqueNode>, Dom<Node>>>,\n52: \n53:     /// A list of pending animation-related events.\n54:     pending_events: DomRefCell<Vec<TransitionOrAnimationEvent>>,\n55: \n56:     /// The timeline value at the last time all animations were marked dirty.\n57:     /// This is used to prevent marking animations dirty when the timeline\n58:     /// has not changed.\n59:     timeline_value_at_last_dirty: Cell<f64>,\n60: }\n61: \n62: impl Animations {\n63:     pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {\n64:         Animations {\n65:             sets: Default::default(),\n66:             has_running_animations: Cell::new(false),\n67:             rooted_nodes: Default::default(),\n68:             pending_events: Default::default(),\n69:             timeline_value_at_last_dirty: Cell::new(0.0),\n70:         }\n71:     }\n72: \n73:     pub(crate) fn clear(&self) {\n74:         self.sets.sets.write().clear();\n75:         self.rooted_nodes.borrow_mut().clear();\n76:         self.pending_events.borrow_mut().clear();\n77:     }\n78: \n79:     // Mark all animations dirty, if they haven't been marked dirty since the\n80:     // specified `current_timeline_value`. Returns true if animations were marked\n81:     // dirty or false otherwise.\n82:     pub(crate) fn mark_animating_nodes_as_dirty(&self, current_timeline_value: f64) -> bool {\n83:         if current_timeline_value <= self.timeline_value_at_last_dirty.get() {\n84:             return false;\n85:         }\n86:         self.timeline_value_at_last_dirty\n87:             .set(current_timeline_value);\n88: \n89:         let sets = self.sets.sets.read();\n90:         let rooted_nodes = self.rooted_nodes.borrow();\n91:         for node in sets\n92:             .keys()\n93:             .filter_map(|key| rooted_nodes.get(&NoTrace(key.node)))\n94:         {\n95:             node.dirty(NodeDamage::Style);\n96:         }\n97: \n98:         true\n99:     }\n100: \n101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99@dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c/-/blob/components/script/animations.rs%3FL1-100"
  }
}

crate::dom::intersectionobserver::IntersectionObserver;\nuse crate::dom::keyboardevent::KeyboardEvent;\nuse crate::dom::largestcontentfulpaint::LargestContentfulPaint;\nuse crate::dom::location::Location;\nuse crate::dom::messageevent::MessageEvent;\nuse crate::dom::mouseevent::MouseEvent;\nuse crate::dom::node::{Node, NodeDamage, NodeFlags, NodeTraits, ShadowIncluding};\nuse crate::dom::nodeiterator::NodeIterator;\nuse crate::dom::nodelist::NodeList;\nuse crate::dom::pagetransitionevent::PageTransitionEvent;\nuse crate::dom::performance::performanceentry::PerformanceEntry;\nuse crate::dom::performance::performancepainttiming::PerformancePaintTiming;\nuse crate::dom::processinginstruction::ProcessingInstruction;\nuse crate::dom::promise::Promise;\nuse crate::dom::range::Range;\nuse crate::dom::resizeobserver::{ResizeObservationDepth, ResizeObserver};\nuse crate::dom::scrolling_box::{ScrollAxisState, ScrollRequirement, ScrollingBox};\nuse crate::dom::selection::Selection;\nuse crate::dom::servoparser::ServoParser;\nuse crate::dom::shadowroot::ShadowRoot;\nuse crate::dom::storageevent::StorageEvent;\nuse crate::dom::text::Text;\nuse crate::dom::touchevent::TouchEvent as DomTouchEvent;\nuse crate::dom::touchlist::TouchList;\nuse crate::dom::treewalker::TreeWalker;\nuse crate::dom::trustedhtml::TrustedHTML;\nuse crate::dom::types::{HTMLCanvasElement, HTMLDialogElement, VisibilityStateEntry};\nuse crate::dom::uievent::UIEvent;\nuse crate::dom::virtualmethods::vtable_for;\nuse crate::dom::websocket::WebSocket;\nuse crate::dom::window::Window;\nuse crate::dom::windowproxy::WindowProxy;\nuse crate::dom::xpathevaluator::XPathEvaluator;\nuse crate::dom::xpathexpression::XPathExpression;\nuse crate::fetch::{DeferredFetchRecordInvokeState, FetchCanceller};\nuse crate::iframe_collection::IFrameCollection;\nuse crate::image_animation::ImageAnimationManager;\nuse crate::messaging::{CommonScriptMsg, MainThreadScriptMsg};\nuse crate::mime::{APPLICATION, CHARSET};\nuse crate::network_listener::{FetchResponseListener, NetworkListener};\nuse crate::realms::{AlreadyInRealm, InRealm, enter_realm};\nuse crate::script_runtime::{CanGc, ScriptThreadEventCategory};\nuse crate::script_thread::ScriptThread;\nuse crate::stylesheet_set::StylesheetSetRef;\nuse crate::task::NonSendTaskBox;\nuse crate::task_source::TaskSourceName;\nuse crate::timers::OneshotTimerCallback;\nuse crate::xpath::parse_expression;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq)]\npub(crate) enum FireMouseEventType {\n    Move,\n    Over,\n    Out,\n    Enter,\n    Leave,\n}\n\nimpl FireMouseEventType {\n    pub(crate) fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        match *self {\n            FireMouseEventType::Move => \"mousemove\",\n            FireMouseEventType::Over => \"mouseover\",\n            FireMouseEventType::Out => \"mouseout\",\n            FireMouseEventType::Enter => \"mouseenter\",\n            FireMouseEventType::Leave => \"mouseleave\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\npub(crate) struct RefreshRedirectDue {\n    #[no_trace]\n    pub(crate) url: ServoUrl,\n    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"non-owning\"]\n    pub(crate) window: DomRoot<Window>,\n}\nimpl RefreshRedirectDue {\n    /// Step 13 of <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#shared-declarative-refresh-steps>\n    pub(crate) fn invoke(self, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        // After the refresh has come due (as defined below),\n        // if the user has not canceled the redirect and, if meta is given,\n        // document's active sandboxing flag set does not have the sandboxed\n        // automatic features browsing context flag set,\n        // then navigate document's node navigable to urlRecord using document,\n        // with historyHandling set to \"replace\".\n        //\n        // TODO: check sandbox\n        // TODO: Check if meta was given\n        let load_data = self\n            .window\n            .load_data_for_document(self.url.clone(), self.window.pipeline_id());\n        self.window\n            .load_url(NavigationHistoryBehavior::Replace, false, load_data, can_gc);\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf, PartialEq)]\npub(crate) enum IsHTMLDocument {\n    HTMLDocument,\n    NonHTMLDocument,\n}\n\n#[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\n#[cfg_attr(crown, crown::unrooted_must_root_lint::must_root)]\nstruct FocusTransaction {\n    /// The focused element of this document.\n    element: Option<Dom<Element>>,\n    /// See [`Document::has_focus`].\n    has_focus: bool,\n    /// Focus options for the transaction\n    focus_options: FocusOptions,\n}\n\n/// Information about a declarative refresh\n#[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\npub(crate) enum DeclarativeRefresh {\n    PendingLoad {\n        #[no_trace]\n        url: ServoUrl,\n        time: u64,\n    },\n    CreatedAfterLoad,\n}\n\n/// Reasons why a [`Document`] might need a rendering update that is otherwise\n/// untracked via other [`Document`] properties.\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\npub(crate) struct RenderingUpdateReason(u8);\n\nbitflags! {\n    impl RenderingUpdateReason: u8 {\n        /// When a `ResizeObserver` starts observing a target, this becomes true, which in turn is a\n        /// signal to the [`ScriptThread`] that a rendering update should happen.\n        const ResizeObserverStartedObservingTarget = 1 << 0;\n        /// When an `IntersectionObserver` starts observing a target, this becomes true, which in turn is a\n        /// signal to the [`ScriptThread`] that a rendering update should happen.\n        const IntersectionObserverStartedObservingTarget = 1 << 1;\n        /// All web fonts have loaded and `fonts.ready` promise has been fulfilled.
